skill.name: comparative-matrix
skill.description: Generate structured comparisons and decision matrices across analyzed frameworks. Use when (1) comparing multiple frameworks or approaches side-by-side, (2) making architectural decisions between alternatives, (3) creating best-of-breed selection documentation, (4) synthesizing findings from multiple analysis skills into actionable decisions, or (5) producing recommendation reports for technical stakeholders.
skill.summary: Create structured comparison matrices and recommendation reports for technical decisions.
skill.author: Dowwie

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backtick execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are false positives from Markdown fences, natural-language description text, and table content in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, network access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration intent were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at multiple SKILL.md lines. These locations are Markdown fenced examples for matrix templates, decision heuristics, and output structure, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged areas are fenced Markdown blocks and contain comparison templates or pseudocode. No command invocation, shell interpreter directive, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md line 3. That line is the skill description for decision matrices and contains no cryptographic algorithm or security implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain metadata text describing comparison and recommendation use cases. I found no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA-1, or any cryptographic operation in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged system reconnaissance at SKILL.md line 49. The referenced line is a comparison-table row about mutable and immutable state, not host inspection or environment discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains framework comparison content only. No hostname, OS, user, network, or filesystem reconnaissance command appears at that location.

content.value_statement: Technical teams often struggle to compare frameworks with consistent criteria. This skill turns analysis findings into structured matrices, trade-off logs, and recommendation documents.
actual_capabilities: Creates side-by-side comparison matrices for multiple frameworks or approaches.
Normalizes findings into common dimensions such as typing, async, state, config, and extensibility.
Applies decision heuristics for scalability, developer experience, and production readiness.
Produces recommendation documents with rationale for technical stakeholders.
Documents trade-offs and compromises behind architecture decisions.
limitations: Requires source analysis or user-provided evidence before it can compare options accurately.
Does not independently benchmark performance or verify claims from external sources.
Focuses on technical frameworks and architecture decisions, not financial procurement scoring.
Recommendations depend on the criteria and priorities supplied by the user.

use_cases.title: Select an Agent Framework
use_cases.description: Compare agent frameworks across typing, async support, memory strategy, tool interfaces, and observability.

use_cases.title: Prepare an Architecture Decision
use_cases.description: Turn research notes into a clear matrix, recommendation, and trade-off log for a review meeting.

use_cases.title: Standardize Evaluation Criteria
use_cases.description: Create reusable comparison dimensions so teams evaluate competing approaches with consistent evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Framework Comparison
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner comparison of two or three options.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare these frameworks using a decision matrix: [framework names]. Focus on typing, async support, state, configuration, and extensibility.

prompt_templates.title: Decision Recommendation
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose one option for a known project.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a recommendation report for [project context]. Compare [options] and explain which option fits our priorities: [priorities].

prompt_templates.title: Evidence-Based Matrix
prompt_templates.scenario: Use existing research notes as source material.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the following analysis notes to build a best-of-breed matrix. Include dimension details, evidence, recommendations, and trade-offs: [analysis notes].

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Review Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Synthesize these framework analyses into a full architecture decision package with a summary matrix, dimension breakdowns, final recommendation, and trade-off log: [analyses].

output_examples.input: Compare three orchestration frameworks for a production agent platform.
output_examples.output: A stakeholder-ready comparison with rows for typing, async behavior, state management, configuration, observability, and tool interfaces. The final section recommends the strongest option and explains the trade-offs.

output_examples.input: Turn research notes into a decision document for choosing between native async and sync wrappers.
output_examples.output: A focused decision matrix that weighs scalability, developer experience, operational risk, and migration cost. It ends with a clear recommendation and conditions where the choice should change.

output_examples.input: Create reusable evaluation criteria for future framework reviews.
output_examples.output: A standard comparison template with core dimensions, domain-specific dimensions, scoring guidance, and prompts for evidence collection.

best_practices: Provide source notes or prior analyses so each comparison cell has evidence.
State decision priorities before asking for a recommendation.
Review the trade-off log with stakeholders before finalizing the decision.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a recommendation without defining project constraints.
Do not treat the matrix as a benchmark when no measurement data was provided.
Do not compare many unrelated options without shared evaluation dimensions.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces comparison matrices, dimension details, recommendation documents, and trade-off logs for technical decisions.

faq.question: Can it compare non-technical products?
faq.answer: It works best for software frameworks, architecture patterns, and engineering approaches. Other comparisons may need custom dimensions.

faq.question: Does it run benchmarks?
faq.answer: No. It can summarize benchmark evidence you provide, but it does not execute tests or collect measurements.

faq.question: How many options can it compare?
faq.answer: It can compare several options, but two to five options usually produce the clearest matrix.

faq.question: What inputs improve the output?
faq.answer: Architecture goals, project constraints, existing analysis notes, and stakeholder priorities improve the quality of recommendations.

faq.question: Can it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. The prompt structure can guide Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through consistent technical comparison work.
